events.title: Queens (NC) Royals vs. Purdue Boilermakers
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Queens (NC) Royals and Purdue Boilermakers on March 20 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Purdue Boilermakers' 97.5% implied probability stems from a stark talent and seeding chasm in this NCAA Tournament first-round clash, pitting the No. 1-seeded Big Ten powerhouse against Queens Royals, a 16th-seeded Division II newcomer making their March Madness debut. Purdue's Zach Edey, the towering two-time National Player of the Year finalist, anchors a squad fresh off a 29-win season with elite rebounding and scoring efficiency, while Queens relies on guard play but lacks the depth and experience against elite competition. No major injuries mar Purdue's official report, and historical 1-vs-16 dominance (35-1 since 1985) bolsters trader consensus. Realistic upset risks include Edey foul trouble, Queens' unheralded shooters erupting for 20+ threes, or Purdue's occasional slow starts snowballing in a neutral-site battle.

This market will resolve to "Up" if the Ethereum price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the ETH/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/eth-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream ETH/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Spread: St. John's Red Storm (-21.5)
description:
In the CBB game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:10 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "St. John's Red Storm" if the St. John's Red Storm win the game by 22 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Northern Iowa Panthers".

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Northern Iowa Panthers vs. St. John's Red Storm
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Northern Iowa Panthers and St. John's Red Storm on March 20 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: St. John's Red Storm's dominant early-season form, including convincing wins over ranked opponents like Baylor, anchors their 82.5% implied probability as heavy favorites against Northern Iowa Panthers. The Red Storm boast superior athleticism and depth under Rick Pitino, with key contributors like RJ Luis Jr. and Deivon Smith excelling in scoring and rebounding, contrasting UNI's mid-major profile marked by inconsistent shooting and defensive lapses in recent MVC tune-ups. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but St. John's home-court edge at Madison Square Garden amplifies matchup dynamics, where they've historically overwhelmed lesser foes. Trader consensus reflects this talent gap, though UNI's gritty upset potential lingers in college hoops volatility.

question: Counter-Strike: MANA eSports vs The Last Resort (BO3) - NODWIN Clutch Series Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 3 match between MANA eSports and The Last Resort in the NODWIN Clutch Series Group Stage, initially scheduled for March 21 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "MANA eSports" if MANA eSports win the match against The Last Resort.

This market will resolve to "The Last Resort" if The Last Resort win the match against MANA eSports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans toward MANA eSports in their BO3 group stage clash with The Last Resort at NODWIN Clutch Series #2, driven by MANA's stronger recent form including a 2-0 sweep over Reckoning Esports and consistent map control on Inferno and Mirage. question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

This market will resolve to "Leo Team" if Leo Team win the match against BIG EQUIPA.

This market will resolve to "BIG EQUIPA" if BIG EQUIPA win the match against Leo Team.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins.

eventMetadata.context_description: BIG EQUIPA holds a strong edge in trader consensus for their BO3 matchup against Leo Team in the NODWIN Clutch Series group stage, reflecting their superior HLTV ranking (top 30) and recent 3-1 win over weaker tier-2 foes, contrasting Leo's inconsistent 2-3 record amid roster flux. Head-to-head favors BIG with a 70% map win rate on shared pools like Mirage and Inferno, where their AWPer tabsen excels. Leo's upset potential hinges on NAF's rifling aggression, but BIG's rest advantage after a bye and home-region crowd boost sentiment toward a 2-0 or 2-1 victory. Watch vetoes for Dust2, Leo's best map, as group implications loom for playoff seeding.

question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Spread: St. John's Red Storm (-18.5)
description:
In the CBB game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:10 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "St. John's Red Storm" if the St. John's Red Storm win the game by 19 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Northern Iowa Panthers".

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Northern Iowa Panthers vs. St. John's Red Storm
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Northern Iowa Panthers and St. John's Red Storm on March 20 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: St. John's Red Storm's dominant early-season form, including convincing wins over ranked opponents like Baylor, anchors their 82.5% implied probability as heavy favorites against Northern Iowa Panthers. The Red Storm boast superior athleticism and depth under Rick Pitino, with key contributors like RJ Luis Jr. and Deivon Smith excelling in scoring and rebounding, contrasting UNI's mid-major profile marked by inconsistent shooting and defensive lapses in recent MVC tune-ups. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but St. John's home-court edge at Madison Square Garden amplifies matchup dynamics, where they've historically overwhelmed lesser foes. Trader consensus reflects this talent gap, though UNI's gritty upset potential lingers in college hoops volatility.

question: UCF Knights vs. UCLA Bruins: O/U 142.5
description:
In the CBB game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:25 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the UCF Knights and UCLA Bruins combine to score 143 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 143, this market will resolve to "Under".

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: UCF Knights vs. UCLA Bruins
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between UCF Knights and UCLA Bruins on March 20 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: UCLA Bruins hold a 67% implied probability as trader consensus in the LA Bowl matchup against UCF Knights largely due to home-field edge at SoFi Stadium and superior roster talent. UCLA enters 5-7 after a gritty late-season push with wins over Washington and Fresno State, showcasing QB Ethan Garbers' efficiency (over 3,000 yards) against UCF's inconsistent secondary. UCF (6-6) faltered on the road, dropping four of five away games amid turnover woes from freshman QB Cam Fancher. No major injuries reported on official updates—both teams near full strength—but UCLA's pass rush (35 sacks) exploits UCF's offensive line vulnerabilities, tilting odds despite bowl unpredictability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Sorana Cirstea a narrow 52.5% implied edge over Elise Mertens in their Miami Open clash, driven by Cirstea's surging hard-court form after reaching the Indian Wells quarterfinals and upsetting higher seeds. The matchup's tight balance arises from Mertens' 5-2 head-to-head advantage, her steady baseline play, and better rest after a straight-sets win, countering Cirstea's aggressive serving on outdoor hard courts. No injuries mar official reports for either, but Miami's variable winds could favor Mertens' superior movement. Momentum swings—like Cirstea's qualifier grind versus Mertens' cleaner path—might tip odds, as early-round upsets often defy slim favorites in WTA 1000 events.
